title: After years of illness, Natick woman runs Boston Marathon with BAA
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Mackenzie Roy, 32, from Natick, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after overcoming autoimmune diseases.
Roy is participating in the marathon with B.A.A. Gives Back, promoting community health and fitness.
The Boston Marathon has been a family tradition for Roy, inspiring her to run the race since childhood.

title: Bruins’ top prospect James Hagens welcomes challenge as he begins pro career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement with the Providence Bruins after his Boston College season ended.
Hagens, the 2025 NHL Draft's seventh overall pick, will start his professional career on Providence's top line.
Providence coach Ryan Mougenel emphasizes growth through AHL competition to prepare Hagens for a future NHL role.

title: What Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White said about their Monday Night Raw appearance
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla and player Derrick White attended WWE Monday Night Raw at TD Garden on Monday.
Mazzulla, an MMA and wrestling enthusiast, enjoyed the event with White and their families, emphasizing relationship-building.
White humorously embraced his 'babyface' role while Mazzulla valued learning from diverse arenas like professional wrestling.

title: Comella’s closes Melrose location after 13 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Comella's has closed its Melrose location after 13 years due to operational and financial challenges.
The closure was a decision made at the franchisee level for the independently owned restaurant on Main St.
Comella's continues to operate 10 other locations across Massachusetts, including Belmont and Concord.

title: The most underrated movies of 2025 are streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Naked Gun' stars Liam Neeson and Pamela Anderson in a 2025 comedy with a script full of jokes. (Paramount+)
'The Ballad of Wallis Island' features Tim Key, Tom Basden, and Carey Mulligan in a reunion comedy-drama. (Prime Video)
'28 Years Later' by Danny Boyle explores Brexit themes in a zombie world, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson and Jodie Comer. (Netflix)

title: Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A Westminster police officer fatally shot a 22-year-old man who allegedly threatened officers with a knife during an arrest.
The incident occurred at the man's residence on South Ashburnham Road around 4:40 p.m. Monday, as officers served an arrest warrant.
Westminster police and State Police detectives are investigating; officers involved are on administrative leave with no reported injuries.

title: Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Dratch's viral moment on 'Celebrity Jeopardy!' involved a fortuitous clue about the Spanish Armada.
Dratch discussed her experience on 'Celebrity Jeopardy!' during an appearance on 'Late Night with Seth Meyers.'
Dratch won $2 million for charity during her appearances in 'Celebrity Jeopardy!' All Stars 2023 and 2024.

title: She survived a brain aneurysm — now she’s finishing the Marathon Majors in Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Andrea Calleja, 46, will complete the Boston Marathon, her final race in the World Marathon Majors.
Calleja survived a ruptured brain aneurysm in 2019, overcoming memory loss, vision impairment, and mobility challenges.
Calleja is an advocate with the Brain Aneurysm Foundation, promoting awareness and support for those with similar struggles.

title: Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 261 at Para Maria is a pop-up bar celebrating women's sports, open at The Envoy Hotel in Boston until April 30.
Named for Kathrine Switzer's Boston Marathon bib number, the bar features sports history exhibits and themed menu items.
A portion of cocktail sales supports Dream Big!, helping girls access sports equipment and leadership training.

title: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay more than $224K over fraud charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to repay over $224,000 in misused pandemic relief and nonprofit donations.
Federal Judge Angel Kelley included $181,000 in diverted donations and fraudulent pandemic benefits in the forfeiture.
Cannon-Grant faces six months home confinement, 100 hours community service, and four years probation for fraud charges.
